transcript: Hello everybody. Uh, I'm the founder of Tapedrive. I want to talk to you today about cloud storage. So let's get started. A few centralized giants hold all of our data. They markup prices way over cost, sell to advertisers, and train their AIs on your data. But that's not all. This year we saw 12 catastrophic outages causing billions of dollars in economic losses. So the moat is eroding, and companies are finally asking the question, what can decentralized infra do for us? If we take a look back, uh, the global revenue for storage was 250 billion last year, over the last year, and is projected to hit 700 billion within a couple years. So there's a huge market opportunity, and the question is, why is decentralized not capturing more of this? Simply put, the current leaders are dropping the ball. With complicated learning curves, um, with with steep learning curves, complicated workflows, and many overlooking small files, which accounts for an absolutely astonishing amount of small uh web and AI data. So, look, this doesn't work. Amazon won because you could create a bucket in 30 seconds and never think about it again. Our uh, so our entire bet is this: Make it actually simple, so that people get convenience, which drives infinite scale. Introducing Tapedrive. Designed to be simple, like S3, it scales with every node, delivers the just works convenience, and unlike others, we're building this uh on the blockchain with the most volume, Solana. So how does it work? Our on-chain program manages payments, staking, consensus, among many other things, while uh the Tape Network stores your data and runs your code. So where are we today? Uh, we've actually knocked out many of the hard issues on the previous slide, uh, including the entirety of the on-chain control plane. Um, but there's a lot more left to do, so please come chat with me afterwards. I'd love to talk. But yeah, uh, with AI demand booming and uh data needs skyrocketing, Tapedrive is poised to lead. If you're building on Solana or need decentralized infra, I'd love to chat. Thank you very much.

enrichment.generated_query: Tapedrive Superteam Demo Day: Tapedrive Breakpoint 2025 announcement

sources.title: Startup Minitap Tops DeepMind’s Mobile AI Benchmark, Raises $4.1 Million Seed Round
sources.snippet:
[Innovation](https://www.forbes.com/innovation/)[Consumer Tech](https://www.forbes.com/consumer-tech/)

# Startup Minitap Tops DeepMind’s Mobile AI Benchmark, Raises $4.1 Million Seed Round

By [Charlie Fink](https://www.forbes.com/sites/charliefink/),

Contributor.

Forbes contributors publish independent expert analyses and insights.
A former tech executive covering AI, XR and The Metaverse for Forbes.

Follow Author

Dec 01, 2025, 07:00am EST

Share

SaveComment

Two twenty three year old founders from a small village in France created [Minitap](https://minitap.ai/), an AI system that outperforms Google DeepMind on its own mobile agent benchmark. They raised a seed round on the strength of that result, and now run a small engineering team between Paris and San Francisco. Founders, Nicolas Dehandschoewercker and his longtime collaborator Luc Mahoux-Nakamura, have been building apps together since high school. Their claim is simple. They say their agent can operate a mobile device more reliably than systems produced by some of the largest AI labs.

Minitap co-founders, Nicolas Dehandschoewercker and Luc Mahoux-Nakamura.

Minitap

AndroidWorld, a benchmark maintained by Google DeepMind, evaluates how well an agent can use a phone across everyday apps. Tasks include scrolling, typing, navigation and functionally complete flows such as booking a ride or sending an email. The benchmark is public. Anyone can download it, wire up an agent and publish results. Dehandschoewercker has posted Minitap’s runs and says the open source version of their framework, called mobile-use, spent the summer near the top of the community leaderboard. No one has to take his word for it. The tests are reproducible, and the code is available under an open license with roughly nineteen hundred stars on GitHub.

This result, and the small team that produced it, is why investors co-led by Moxxie Ventures and Mercuri, with participation from EWOR, Tekton Ventures, Amigos Venture Capital, and six unicorn founders funded the company only a few months after it formed. The £4.1m seed round closed in October.. Minitap’s headcount remains small. Most of its engineers are in Paris. The founders work between California and France, flying teams back and forth as needed. When I spoke with Dehandschoewercker he was in Tokyo, where the group had gathered temporarily to work through deadlines.

Minitap design verification.

Minitap

The benchmark is not the business, but it matters because of the problem Minitap is trying to solve. Mobile engineers still write Swift, Kotlin or React Native code without meaningful feedback from AI tools. Copilots can generate patches, but they cannot see the phone, understand layout problems or verify what they built. If something is off by a few pixels or an interaction fails, the engineer has to step in. Dehandschoewercker says that is why building consumer mobile apps still takes weeks even when AI produces some of the code.

MORE FOR YOU

Minitap’s agent connects to a virtual phone in the cloud. It can see the screen, compare it to a Figma design, test interactions and report failures. This depends on the same capabilities measured by AndroidWorld. The user experience for developers is closer to web testing tools, where an agent can interact with the real page and validate outcomes. Dehandschoewercker wants mobile development to move at the same pace. The company is working with early design partners to refine pricing and deployment models.

Several groups are working on agentic QA or device control, though they approach the problem from different parts of the pipeline. Momentic and Autosana focus on automated testing for QA teams. Their tools generate natural language test cases and maintain them as the app changes. AskUI has built a vision driven controller for desktop and mobile interfaces. These companies run agents against apps and browsers, but they do not position themselves as inner loop development tools. BrowserStack remai

# ![LOGO_horizontal (1).png](https://ml-eu.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/9b76ef2d-5df1-485e-a489-d0691cbaedb2?size=3)
# Minitap Raises $4.1M to Make Mobile Development 10x Faster with AI
## Two 23-year-olds from rural France beat Google DeepMind on industry benchmark within 40 days, backed by six unicorn founders including Hugging Face and SumUp
December 01, 2025 08:00 ET| Source:[Minitap](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/Minitap)FollowMinitap
Share
* * * * * * * * San Francisco, Dec. 01, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --[Minitap](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=vlptYOpDCZU1nikjqpa70s5sL64cBuJ0hbl-G9VSzG1y2s46o8IdWekkrpaXyIjr7sNG8oG1RGU38Kf4iCBufQ==), the AI-powered mobile development platform, today announced it has raised $4.1 million in seed funding co-led by Moxxie Ventures and Mercuri, with participation from EWOR, Tekton Ventures, Amigos Venture Capital, and six unicorn founders. The round comes just four months after Minitap founders achieved the #1 position globally on AndroidWorld, the industry benchmark for AI-controlled mobile devices, surpassing research teams from Google DeepMind, ByteDance, Microsoft Research, and Alibaba.
![](https://ml-eu.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/b5847a9d-bf8c-482e-a926-1e349cfd5f88/picture-to-post.jpg "PICTURE_TO_POST.jpg")[](#)
*Minitap founders: Nicolas Dehandschoewercker and Luc Mahoux-Nakamura.*
Mobile development is 10x slower than web development, even in the age of AI. While tools like Cursor and Claude enable web developers to ship features in 2 days that previously took 2 weeks, those same AI tools remain largely ineffective for mobile—unable to test on devices, iterate when things break, or verify features work across configurations. Minitapsolves this bottleneck, enabling engineering teams to build mobile features in days, instead of the usual six weeks.
"We spent two years building our first viral mobile product, today, and I'm embarrassed by that timeline," said**Nicolas Dehandschoewercker, co-founder and CEO of Minitap**. "Mobile is 60% of internet usage but moves at 10% of web speed. Every consumer app company (Duolingo, Calm, Hinge etc) ships 5x more experiments on web than mobile. We built Minitapto close that gap for everyone."
![](https://ml-eu.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/ebaa1099-0d56-47e7-8be1-233d12b1218d/mcp-design-verification.png "mcp_design_verification.png")[](#)
*Minitap in action.*
"Every consumer mobile company needs to experiment faster," added**Luc Mahoux-Nakamura, co-founder at Minitap.**"The companies that run 10x more experiments will win their markets. We're building the infrastructure that makes that speed possible."
Nico and Luc met in Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire, a village in Burgundy, France. Nico had spent two years in military school; Luc was a child prodigy. Luc taught Nico how to code, and they studied together every day to rank in France’s top 0.1% academically. Seven years later, they’ve built every project side by side.
At 18, they created their first mobile app and bootstrapped Fuego to 10,000 users. Nico went on to study Biomedical Engineering at Imperial College London and carry out AI research, obsessed with DeepMind's AlphaGo documentary. Luc built delivery drone infrastructure at Rakuten. Together, they combined mobile development experience, AI research expertise, and infrastructure built at scale, a combination no research lab could match.
“Nicolas is leading one of the fastest teams I’ve seen. It comes from years of working together,

# ‘Township Tale’ Studio’s New VR Extraction Dungeon Crawler Launches Open Playtests This Week
By
[Scott Hayden](https://www.roadtovr.com/author/scotthayden/)
-
Dec 1, 2025
[**9](https://www.roadtovr.com/vr-extraction-dungeon-crawler-reave-playtest-release-date/#comments)
Alta, the studio behind*A Township Tale (2021)*, is getting ready to launch open playtesting for its upcoming dungeon-crawler extraction game*REAVE.*
### The News
While*Reave*has technically had a number of playtests starting earlier this year, they’ve required users to sign up for the game’s Discord ([invite link](https://discord.com/invite/Reave)) and get approval first.
Now, the studio is opening up playtesting to anyone with the launch of*Reave: Limited Access,*which is coming to Quest on December 4th. You can find it[on the Horizon Store](https://www.meta.com/en-gb/experiences/reave/5961656013961118/)for Quest 3,[a special invite](https://www.oculus.com/experiences/5961656013961118/release-channels/858141529966475/?token=9MO1gkpv)for Quest 2, and also on[Steam](https://s

tags.impact_reason: Tapedrive is a new decentralized storage solution on Solana, which is a solid update, but it's still in early stages and needs to demonstrate adoption to have a major impact.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the futuristic tape drive server rack as the anchor because it directly references the company's name, 'Tapedrive', while modernizing the concept to fit with high-tech cloud storage on the Solana blockchain. The visual bridges the gap between a familiar storage metaphor and its decentralized, futuristic application. I included the text '$700B' as a holographic display on the server rack to diegetically represent the massive market opportunity Tapedrive is targeting, a key point from the transcript.
visual_idea.description: A dramatic macro shot focusing on a single, operating tape drive. The camera is tight on the reel-to-reel mechanism, showing a translucent tape filled with glowing, fiber-optic-like threads of data in Solana's colors. The tape spools gracefully from one chrome reel to another, creating a mesmerizing visual of data in motion. The lighting is focused and cinematic, highlighting the polished metal textures and the internal glow of the data tape.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A dramatic macro shot focusing on a single, operating tape drive, composed for a square 1:1 aspect ratio with precise symmetry and central focus, entirely filling the frame with rich texture. The camera is tight on the reel-to-reel mechanism, meticulously highlighting the polished metal textures as Anodized Aluminum. A translucent tape, crafted from Glowing Acrylic, is shown filled with intensely glowing, fiber-optic-like threads of data in Solana's distinct colors (purple, green, yellow). This tape spools gracefully from one chrome reel to another, creating a mesmerizing visual of data in motion. The lighting is focused and cinematic, featuring dramatic Volumetric Lighting that casts ethereal rays and enhances the internal glow of the data tape, evoking the deep, organized atmosphere of 'The Archive'. Every surface showcases hyper-detailed, microscopic textures with realistic materials. 8k resolution, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, physically based rendering (PBR), cinematic contrast.

editorial.headline: Colosseum Winner Tapedrive Unveils 'S3-Native' Storage on Solana
editorial.standfirst: Founder Zelimir Fedoran targets the $700B cloud storage market by solving the 'usability gap' in DePIN, promising S3-like simplicity for the high-frequency data demands of AI and Web3.
key_takeaways: Tapedrive claims a 1,400x cost reduction versus standard on-chain storage by decoupling the Solana control plane from the 'Tape Network' data plane.
The protocol specifically targets 'small file' workloads—critical for AI datasets and web hosting—distinguishing it from archival-focused competitors like Arweave.
Development is led by Zelimir Fedoran, whose project previously secured the Grand Prize at the Colosseum Hackathon.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
### The 'S3 Moment' for Decentralized Storage

While the decentralized physical infrastructure network (DePIN) narrative has matured, decentralized storage has largely failed to displace incumbents like AWS S3 due to a critical friction point: User Experience. In his address at the Superteam Demo Day, **Tapedrive** founder Zelimir Fedoran identified this "complexity moat" as the primary barrier preventing decentralized protocols from capturing a slice of the projected $700 billion global storage market. Unlike **Filecoin** or **Arweave**, which often cater to cold archival or complex permanent storage needs, Tapedrive is engineering a solution focused purely on developer velocity—creating a bucket in 30 seconds and forgetting about it.

### Architecture: Decoupling for Scale

The technical "alpha" lies in Tapedrive's architectural split. Rather than clogging the Solana mainnet with raw data, the protocol utilizes Solana exclusively as a high-throughput **Control Plane**—managing payments, staking, and consensus. The actual data resides on the **Tape Network**, a specialized layer designed for storage and code execution. This hybrid approach allows Tapedrive to leverage Solana’s speed for state management while achieving a claimed **1,400x reduction in storage costs** compared to standard on-chain methods. By anchoring writes to the Solana ledger without storing the payloads there, Tapedrive maintains cryptographic verifiability without the bloating costs.

### The AI Data Supply Chain

Fedoran’s pivot toward "small files" is a strategic bet on the needs of the burgeoning AI sector. Current decentralized storage leaders are optimized for large, static blobs. However, modern AI and web applications require rapid retrieval of millions of small files—weights, biases, and JSON configurations. If Tapedrive can deliver on its promise of a "just works" convenience layer, it positions Solana not just as a transactional layer for DeFi, but as the active data warehouse for the next generation of on-chain AI agents.
